eyebrow: Cinema Palette · Sourced from the frame
tagline: Forty-eight films, read for their colour grade, not their poster.
metaNote:
Color sourced from grade,
production design & film stock
sourcing: Every palette is sampled from a specific film's actual colour grade, production design, lighting, and film stock — the colour the cinematographer and designer built into the frame. Iconic works are named; the aim is the palette you would pull from a freeze-frame, not a generic 'cinematic' look.

volumes.title: Film Noir & Shadow
volumes.eyebrow: Cinema · Vol I · Sourced from the Frame
volumes.h1: Four palettes from the shadow pictures, tinted silver and smoke.
preface: Classic noir is read as black-and-white, but the silver-nitrate frame is never neutral — it tilts warm toward tungsten or cool toward night, and the eye reads cigarette smoke, wet asphalt, and venetian-blind shadow as distinct greys. The palette is a near-monochrome with one or two tints doing all the work.
Each refuses the flat black-and-white assumption: the noir frame is a tinted grey scale, not an absence of colour.

palettes.kicker: Double Indemnity · 1944 · dir. Billy Wilder · the venetian-blind living room
palettes.title: Afternoon light through slatted blinds, striping a room where the deal is already done
palettes.source: Color from the high-contrast noir frame: tungsten-warm plaster grey, venetian-blind shadow, cigarette smoke, a black telephone, and the cool grey of a tailored suit.

swatches.name: Blind shadow, charcoal
swatches.note: The signature slatted dark across the wall.

swatches.name: Cigarette smoke, pale silver
swatches.note: Haze hanging in the key light.

swatches.name: Lipstick, muted oxblood
swatches.note: The one warm read on a femme fatale.

d.text: Warm plaster grey is the lit interior field.

s.text: Blind shadow, smoke silver, and suit grey.

a.text: Black props and a single oxblood as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Pure black-and-white. The nitrate print is warm-tinted; flat neutral grey loses the tungsten heat of the room.

palettes.kicker: The Third Man · 1949 · dir. Carol Reed · the wet Vienna cobbles at night
palettes.title: A canted night street in occupied Vienna, cobbles slick under a single lamp
palettes.source: Color from Reed's tilted night: black wet cobbles, a halo of warm sodium lamp, fog grey, rubble-shadow charcoal, and the brief gleam of a doorway.

swatches.name: Wet cobbles, black-grey
swatches.note: Rain-slicked stone reflecting the one lamp.

swatches.name: Lamp halo, sodium gold
swatches.note: The lone street lamp's warm pool.

swatches.name: Doorway gleam, pale grey
swatches.note: A figure's reveal in a recessed door.

swatches.name: Zither-warm interior, amber
swatches.note: A glimpsed lit window, warm and rare.

d.text: Black wet cobbles dominate the night street.

s.text: Sodium lamp, fog silver, and rubble charcoal.

a.text: Doorway grey and amber window as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Even grey-scale. Reed's Vienna is built on one warm lamp against pervasive black-blue; flat grey kills the contrast.

palettes.kicker: The Night of the Hunter · 1955 · dir. Charles Laughton · the river drift
palettes.title: A child's boat drifting downriver under a fairy-tale moon, expressionist and stark
palettes.source: Color from Laughton's expressionist frame: moon-silver river, black reed silhouette, pale night sky, the white of a nightgown, and the deep black of menace approaching.

swatches.name: Moonlit river, silver-grey
swatches.note: The dreamlike river under a stage moon.

swatches.name: Reed silhouette, black
swatches.note: Bulrushes cut flat against the light.

swatches.name: Nightgown, chalk white
swatches.note: The children's clothes glowing in the dark.

swatches.name: Approaching dark, ink black
swatches.note: The preacher's shape on the horizon.

swatches.name: Lamp-lit cabin, warm grey
swatches.note: A safe window glimpsed from the water.

d.text: Silver moonlit river is the dominant field.

s.text: Black reeds, ash sky, and chalk nightgown.

a.text: Ink-black menace and warm cabin as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Naturalistic grey. The film is a fable in pure silver and black; mid-tone realism would flatten its storybook contrast.

palettes.kicker: Touch of Evil · 1958 · dir. Orson Welles · the border-town night
palettes.title: A sweaty Mexican-border night of neon, oil derricks, and deep-focus shadow
palettes.source: Color from Welles's border noir: oily black night, blown-out arc-lamp white, motel-neon grey, dust-grey street, and a flicker of bar neon.

swatches.name: Bar neon, dirty cyan
swatches.note: A cantina sign bleeding into the dark.

swatches.name: Derrick flame, ember
swatches.note: Oil-field fire on the night horizon.

d.text: Oily black night dominates the long takes.

s.text: Arc-white, motel silver, and dust grey.

a.text: Bar-neon cyan and derrick ember as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Clean studio monochrome. Welles's border is grimy, blown-out, and neon-flecked — a dirty grey scale, not a polished one.

volumes.title: Technicolor Golden Age
volumes.eyebrow: Cinema · Vol II · Sourced from the Frame
volumes.h1: Four palettes from three-strip Technicolor, saturated past life.
preface: Three-strip Technicolor produced colour more saturated and separated than reality — primary reds, emerald greens, and impossible skies, all under bright, even key light. The palettes are bold, clean, and joyful, with deep saturation as the whole point.
Each refuses the muddy desaturated 'classic film' assumption: golden-age Technicolor is the most saturated colour cinema ever made.

palettes.kicker: The Wizard of Oz · 1939 · the gates of the Emerald City
palettes.title: The shift to colour: a yellow-brick road running to an impossible green city
palettes.source: Color from the most famous colour reveal: yellow-brick gold, emerald-city green, ruby-slipper red, poppy-field scarlet, and a painted cobalt sky.

swatches.name: Yellow brick, Technicolor gold
swatches.note: The road's saturated paving, brighter than life.

swatches.name: Poppy field, scarlet
swatches.note: The sleeping field's mass of red.

swatches.name: Ruby slippers, sequin red
swatches.note: The film's most famous saturated prop.

swatches.name: Gingham, cornflower blue
swatches.note: Dorothy's dress, a softer blue note.

d.text: Yellow-brick gold leads the eye down the road.

s.text: Emerald green, poppy scarlet, and cobalt sky.

a.text: Ruby slippers and gingham blue as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Faded-vintage wash. Technicolor was hyper-saturated by design; the sepia-nostalgia filter is the opposite of its intent.

palettes.kicker: The Red Shoes · 1948 · dir. Powell & Pressburger · the ballet
palettes.title: A surreal ballet sequence ablaze in red against painted theatrical sets
palettes.source: Color from the Archers' Technicolor: the famous red shoes scarlet, stage-curtain crimson, footlight gold, painted-set teal, and a dancer's white tulle.

swatches.name: Red shoes, vivid scarlet
swatches.note: The cursed shoes, the film's burning centre.

swatches.name: Stage curtain, deep crimson
swatches.note: Velvet theatre drapes framing the dance.

swatches.name: Tulle skirt, chalk white
swatches.note: The ballerina's costume against the red.

d.text: Scarlet red dominates the ballet's palette.

s.text: Crimson curtain, footlight gold, and teal set.

a.text: White tulle and plum shadow as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Muted period drama. The Archers used Technicolor expressionistically; restraint betrays the film's feverish red.

palettes.kicker: Singin' in the Rain · 1952 · the 'Broadway Melody' set
palettes.title: A musical-comedy soundstage of canary yellow, candy pink, and saturated primaries
palettes.source: Color from MGM's musical Technicolor: canary-yellow raincoat, candy-pink set, primary-blue suit, lawn green, and bright lipstick red.

swatches.name: Raincoat, canary yellow
swatches.note: Kelly's bright slicker in the title number.

swatches.name: Tailored suit, primary blue
swatches.note: Saturated costume blue under even key.

swatches.name: Umbrella, glossy black
swatches.note: The prop umbrella against the colour.

d.text: Canary yellow leads the musical's palette.

s.text: Candy pink, primary blue, and bright green.

a.text: Vivid lipstick and black umbrella as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Nostalgic desaturation. The MGM musical is pure saturated joy; dulling it removes the candy-bright optimism.

palettes.kicker: Vertigo · 1958 · dir. Alfred Hitchcock · the green neon hotel
palettes.title: A San Francisco obsession bathed in unnatural green and feverish red
palettes.source: Color from Hitchcock's Technicolor unease: eerie neon green, feverish red, grey suit, golden hair, and the deep blue of the bay at dusk.

swatches.name: Hotel neon, eerie green
swatches.note: The Empire Hotel sign bathing the room.

swatches.name: Obsession red, feverish
swatches.note: The recurring saturated red of dread.

swatches.name: Madeleine's hair, gold
swatches.note: The famous spiral chignon, warm gold.

swatches.name: Bay dusk, deep blue
swatches.note: San Francisco water at the blue hour.

swatches.name: Bouquet, antique violet
swatches.note: The nosegay of the dead woman.

d.text: Eerie neon green dominates the obsession scenes.

s.text: Feverish red, grey suit, and gold hair.

a.text: Bay blue and violet bouquet as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Naturalistic colour. Hitchcock weaponised Technicolor green and red as psychology; realism drains the dread.

volumes.title: New Hollywood & the Grain
volumes.eyebrow: Cinema · Vol III · Sourced from the Frame
volumes.h1: Four palettes from the 1970s, warm, grainy, and underlit.
preface: The New Hollywood look came from fast film stock, available light, and a deliberate rejection of studio gloss — warm tungsten interiors, smoky browns, and underexposed shadow, with grain as a texture. The palettes are earthy and low-key.
Each refuses the clean modern grade: the seventies frame is warm, dim, and amber, not crisp and cool.

palettes.kicker: The Godfather · 1972 · dir. Coppola · cin. Gordon Willis · the don's study
palettes.title: A study lit only from above, faces falling into protective shadow over warm wood
palettes.source: Color from Willis's 'prince of darkness' lighting: deep shadow brown, warm tungsten amber, mahogany wood, sallow skin highlight, and a blood-orange of Sicily in flashback.

swatches.name: Mahogany desk, red-brown
swatches.note: Polished dark wood of the office.

swatches.name: Skin highlight, sallow gold
swatches.note: The only lit part of a face.

swatches.name: Blood, dark crimson
swatches.note: The violence, kept deep and rare.

d.text: Deep brown-black shadow dominates the interiors.

s.text: Tungsten amber, mahogany, and sallow skin.

a.text: Sicilian orange and dark blood as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Bright even lighting. Willis built the film on shadow and warmth; flat fill destroys the don's menace.

palettes.kicker: Taxi Driver · 1976 · dir. Scorsese · the neon city through a windshield
palettes.title: A Manhattan night seen through steam and a cab windshield, smeared red and amber
palettes.source: Color from the city's fever: steam-lit red neon, sodium-amber street, wet-black asphalt, sickly green light, and a checker-cab yellow.

swatches.name: Checker cab, taxi yellow
swatches.note: The yellow hood through the rain.

d.text: Black wet asphalt is the night's base.

s.text: Red neon, sodium amber, and sick green.

a.text: Cab yellow and steam white as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Clean neon glamour. Scorsese's city is smeared, sweaty, and sick-lit — not the polished neon of a music video.

palettes.kicker: McCabe & Mrs. Miller · 1971 · dir. Altman · the snowbound mining town
palettes.title: A flashed, fogged frontier town in the rain and snow of the Pacific Northwest
palettes.source: Color from Altman's pre-flashed stock: muted fog grey-gold, raw timber brown, lamp-lit window amber, snow-grey, and the dull green of wet fir.

swatches.name: Window lamp, amber
swatches.note: Warm interior light in the gloom.

d.text: Flashed grey-gold fog softens the whole film.

s.text: Raw timber, amber windows, and wet snow.

a.text: Dull fir and opium amber as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Crisp HD clarity. Altman fogged the negative for a faded, rain-soaked memory; sharp clean colour erases the mood.

palettes.kicker: Apocalypse Now · 1979 · dir. Coppola · the river at dusk
palettes.title: A boat moving upriver into orange smoke and green jungle toward the dark
palettes.source: Color from the war's fever-dream: napalm-orange sky, dense jungle green, brown river, purple smoke, and the deep shadow of Kurtz's compound.

swatches.name: Jungle green, dense
swatches.note: The wall of vegetation along the river.

swatches.name: River, muddy brown
swatches.note: The Nung river carrying the boat.

d.text: Dense jungle green frames the whole river.

s.text: Napalm orange, muddy river, and purple smoke.

a.text: Ink compound and ember fire as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Cool war-film grade. Coppola's war is hot, green, and orange — a hallucination, not a desaturated battlefield.

volumes.title: Science-Fiction Futures
volumes.eyebrow: Cinema · Vol IV · Sourced from the Frame
volumes.h1: Four palettes from imagined futures, cool, clean, and vast.
preface: Cinematic sci-fi tends to a controlled palette: sterile whites and blacks for the rational future, a single saturated accent for the technology, and vast cool fields for scale. The colour signals order, then breaks it with one hue.
Each refuses the cluttered rainbow: the future on film is disciplined, usually monochrome-plus-one.

palettes.kicker: 2001: A Space Odyssey · 1968 · dir. Kubrick · the centrifuge & the stargate
palettes.title: A pristine white ship interior, then the eye of HAL red, then the stargate's colour storm
palettes.source: Color from Kubrick's future: clinical white interior, HAL-eye red, black space void, control-panel amber, and a flash of stargate magenta.

swatches.name: Stargate, electric magenta
swatches.note: A flash from the psychedelic finale.

d.text: Clinical white is the rational future's field.

s.text: Space black, amber panels, and cabin grey.

a.text: HAL red and stargate magenta as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Busy retro-future colour. Kubrick's future is disciplined white-and-black; the colour storm is reserved for transcendence.

palettes.kicker: Blade Runner 2049 · 2017 · dir. Villeneuve · cin. Deakins · the Vegas ruins
palettes.title: A dust-choked orange void where two figures meet in a derelict casino
palettes.source: Color from Deakins's saturated blocks: irradiated orange haze, ash grey, holographic pink, deep teal night, and a sliver of warm gold.

swatches.name: Vegas haze, irradiated orange
swatches.note: The dust-storm monochrome of the ruins.

d.text: Irradiated orange haze fills the Vegas scenes.

s.text: Ash grey, teal night, and hologram pink.

a.text: Cyan glow and whisky gold as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Teal-and-orange everywhere. Deakins commits to one saturated colour per location; mixing them muddies his blocks.

palettes.kicker: Arrival · 2016 · dir. Villeneuve · the shell interior
palettes.title: A misty grey-green chamber where humans face a fog-veiled alien presence
palettes.source: Color from the film's muted unease: fog grey-green, charcoal shell, hazmat orange, pale cream mist, and the ink-black of the heptapod's logogram.

swatches.name: Chamber fog, grey-green
swatches.note: The cold misted air of the contact room.

swatches.name: Hazmat suit, safety orange
swatches.note: The team's protective gear, the one warm read.

d.text: Grey-green fog dominates the contact scenes.

s.text: Charcoal shell, cream mist, and slate sky.

a.text: Hazmat orange and logogram ink as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Bright sci-fi spectacle. Arrival is hushed and desaturated; the one warm accent is the human suit, deliberately alone.

palettes.kicker: TRON: Legacy · 2010 · dir. Kosinski · the Grid
palettes.title: A black digital world cut by glowing lines of cyan and orange
palettes.source: Color from the Grid: pure black void, program cyan, antagonist orange, white edge-light, and a cool interface blue.

swatches.name: The Grid, pure black
swatches.note: The black digital floor and sky.

d.text: Pure black is the Grid's infinite field.

s.text: Cyan glow, interface blue, and white edges.

a.text: CLU orange and derez aqua as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Daylight realism. The Grid only exists as glow-on-black; any ambient fill collapses its digital depth.

volumes.title: Horror & the Uncanny
volumes.eyebrow: Cinema · Vol V · Sourced from the Frame
volumes.h1: Four palettes from the dread pictures, where colour does the scaring.
preface: Horror uses colour as threat: an aggressive saturated hue against an otherwise muted field, or a sickly cast that makes the ordinary wrong. The structure is often a calm base violated by one alarming colour.
Each refuses the generic dark-and-grey 'scary' look: the most frightening horror palettes are specific and often lurid.

palettes.kicker: Suspiria · 1977 · dir. Argento · the ballet academy
palettes.title: A witches' academy drenched in primary red and electric blue, candy-coloured and lethal
palettes.source: Color from Argento's Technicolor nightmare: aggressive blood red, electric blue, acid green, deep shadow black, and a gold art-nouveau trim.

swatches.name: Corridor green, acid
swatches.note: Sickly green of the deep halls.

swatches.name: Shadow, deep black
swatches.note: The dark the colour pools against.

d.text: Blood red floods the academy's key scenes.

s.text: Electric blue, acid green, and black shadow.

a.text: Gold trim and pallid white as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Muted modern horror. Argento scares with saturated candy colour; desaturation removes the lurid threat.

palettes.kicker: The Shining · 1980 · dir. Kubrick · the Overlook Hotel
palettes.title: A vast empty hotel of warm carpet, cold daylight, and one wall of elevator blood
palettes.source: Color from the Overlook: hexagonal-carpet orange, cold daylight grey, bathroom-red, teal-green walls, and the flat gold of the Gold Room.

swatches.name: Daylight, cold grey
swatches.note: Flat overcast through the big windows.

swatches.name: Corridor wall, teal green
swatches.note: The unsettling green of the halls.

d.text: Burnt-orange carpet dominates the corridors.

s.text: Cold daylight, teal walls, and deep red.

a.text: Gold Room and salmon tile as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Dark gloomy haunted-house. The Overlook's terror is bright, symmetrical, and warm — emptiness in full light.

palettes.kicker: Hereditary · 2018 · dir. Aster · the dollhouse home
palettes.title: A grief-stricken family home of warm wood and shadow, dread building in the corners
palettes.source: Color from the film's domestic dread: warm wood-brown interior, deep shadow, sickly miniature-lamp amber, muted teal, and a flash of fire orange.

swatches.name: Lamp amber, sickly warm
swatches.note: Practical lamps in the dim rooms.

swatches.name: Cool wall, muted teal
swatches.note: Daylit rooms with a cold cast.

swatches.name: Treehouse fire, ember orange
swatches.note: The climactic flame in the dark.

d.text: Warm wood-brown is the home's oppressive field.

s.text: Black corners, sickly amber, and cold teal.

a.text: Ember fire and pallid skin as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Jump-scare neon. Aster's dread is slow, warm, and domestic; the horror hides in ordinary lamplight, not flash.

palettes.kicker: The Witch · 2015 · dir. Eggers · the farm at the wood's edge
palettes.title: A bleak Puritan farmstead in flat New England daylight against a black forest
palettes.source: Color from Eggers's natural-light dread: desaturated grey daylight, mud brown, homespun cream, black forest, and a single read of blood.

swatches.name: Forest, black-green
swatches.note: The wall of trees harbouring evil.

swatches.name: Blood, dark red
swatches.note: The one saturated read in a grey film.

d.text: Desaturated grey daylight dominates the farm.

s.text: Cold mud, dim homespun, and black forest.

a.text: Dark blood and weak candle as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Stylised colour horror. Eggers uses only period-plausible natural light; saturation would break the bleak realism.

volumes.title: The Western Frontier
volumes.eyebrow: Cinema · Vol VI · Sourced from the Frame
volumes.h1: Four palettes from the frontier, sun-scorched and dust-blown.
preface: The Western lives in hard sunlight and dust: bleached sky, ochre earth, leather, and the deep shadow of a hat brim. Its palette is warm, dry, and high-contrast, set by the specific desert and stock used.
Each refuses the generic 'sepia old-timey' wash: real Western grades are vivid ochre-and-blue or bleached white, not uniform brown.

palettes.kicker: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly · 1966 · dir. Leone · the desert duel
palettes.title: A three-way standoff in a circular graveyard under a hard blue sky
palettes.source: Color from the spaghetti Western: bleached desert ochre, hard cobalt sky, leather brown, dust haze, and a poncho's faded red.

swatches.name: Poncho, faded brick red
swatches.note: The Man with No Name's wrap.

d.text: Bleached ochre earth is the duel's ground.

s.text: Cobalt sky, leather brown, and dust tan.

a.text: Faded poncho red and blue steel as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Uniform sepia. Leone pairs hot ochre against saturated blue; a single brown wash flattens the standoff.

palettes.kicker: There Will Be Blood · 2007 · dir. P.T. Anderson · the oil derrick fire
palettes.title: A turn-of-century California oilfield, dust and derricks under a vast pale sky
palettes.source: Color from the oil West: dust-ochre earth, derrick-fire orange, oil black, pale washed sky, and the brown of a wool suit.

swatches.name: Derrick fire, orange
swatches.note: The blowout burning against the night.

d.text: Dust-ochre earth is the film's dry field.

s.text: Washed sky, drab suit, and slick oil.

a.text: Derrick fire and dark blood as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Warm-nostalgia gold. Anderson's West is harsh, pale, and dusty; the only heat is fire and blood.

palettes.kicker: The Revenant · 2015 · dir. Iñárritu · cin. Lubezki · the frozen wilderness
palettes.title: A natural-light survival epic in blue-grey snow, black forest, and breath-fog
palettes.source: Color from Lubezki's available light: blue-grey snow, black-green forest, icy river steel, pale overcast sky, and a low ember of campfire.

swatches.name: Campfire, low ember
swatches.note: The one warmth in the cold frame.

d.text: Blue-grey snow dominates the survival frame.

s.text: Black forest, steel river, and pale sky.

a.text: Ember fire and dried blood as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Warm adventure grade. Shot in daylight only, the film is cold and blue; warmth survives only as a dying fire.

palettes.kicker: Once Upon a Time in the West · 1968 · dir. Leone · the railhead town
palettes.title: A dusty frontier town built around a coming railroad, gold light and long shadow
palettes.source: Color from Leone's epic: golden dust light, raw timber, duster-coat tan, deep shadow, and the turquoise of a woman's dress.

swatches.name: Dust light, golden tan
swatches.note: Warm low sun through the haze.

swatches.name: Deep shadow, brown-black
swatches.note: The dark under hat brims and porches.

swatches.name: Jill's dress, dusty turquoise
swatches.note: The one cool colour in the town.

d.text: Golden dust light suffuses the whole town.

s.text: Raw timber, pale dusters, and deep shadow.

a.text: Turquoise dress and steel as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Flat brown nostalgia. Leone's gold light against deep shadow and one turquoise is operatic, not a sepia snapshot.

volumes.title: Asian Art Cinema
volumes.eyebrow: Cinema · Vol VII · Sourced from the Frame
volumes.h1: Four palettes from the Asian masters, where colour is composition.
preface: Across Hong Kong, mainland China, Korea, and beyond, a strain of art cinema treats colour as deliberate composition — saturated single-hue rooms, costume as palette, and light filtered through period detail. The colour is rich, controlled, and emotional.
Each refuses the flat 'foreign film' assumption: these are among the most consciously colour-designed films ever made.

palettes.kicker: In the Mood for Love · 2000 · dir. Wong Kar-wai · the noodle-stall corridor
palettes.title: Two neighbours pass in a narrow corridor, her cheongsam burning against shadow
palettes.source: Color from Wong's romance: deep shadow green-black, cheongsam crimson, golden lamp, jade-green wall, and a smoky amber.

swatches.name: Corridor shadow, green-black
swatches.note: The dim, smoky stairwells and halls.

swatches.name: Smoke, amber haze
swatches.note: Cigarette smoke in the lamp light.

d.text: Green-black shadow is the field the colour glows against.

s.text: Crimson cheongsam, gold lamp, and jade wall.

a.text: Amber smoke and mustard curtain as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Bright clean melodrama. Wong's romance lives in smoky shadow lit by one crimson dress; flat light kills the longing.

palettes.kicker: Hero · 2002 · dir. Zhang Yimou · the red courtyard duel
palettes.title: A duel among falling autumn leaves, the entire frame a single flooding colour
palettes.source: Color from Zhang's chromatic chapters: flooding red, autumn-leaf gold, calligraphy black, jade-lake green, and silk white.

swatches.name: Flooding red, vivid
swatches.note: The red chapter's costumes and drapes.

swatches.name: Autumn leaves, gold-amber
swatches.note: The swirling leaves of the duel.

d.text: Vivid red floods the courtyard chapter.

s.text: Gold leaves, ink black, and jade lake.

a.text: Silk white and banner blue as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Muted realism. Zhang structures the film as monochrome chapters; restraint betrays the chromatic storytelling.

palettes.kicker: Oldboy · 2003 · dir. Park Chan-wook · the corridor & the apartment
palettes.title: A revenge thriller of bruised purples, sickly greens, and a famous green-toned corridor
palettes.source: Color from Park's stylised palette: bruise purple, sickly fluorescent green, deep maroon, slate grey, and a violent red.

d.text: Muted bruise purple casts the whole film.

s.text: Sickly green, deep maroon, and slate grey.

a.text: Hot violence red and neon magenta as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Neutral thriller grade. Park's revenge is colour-sick — purple and green bruising every frame, not clean realism.

palettes.kicker: Raise the Red Lantern · 1991 · dir. Zhang Yimou · the courtyard at night
palettes.title: A great house where red lanterns mark the master's favour against grey stone
palettes.source: Color from the Qiao compound: lantern red, cold grey-stone courtyard, blue-grey night, lamp gold, and the indigo of servants' dress.

swatches.name: Lamp glow, warm gold
swatches.note: Interior light from the lit chamber.

swatches.name: Frost roof, pale grey
swatches.note: The snow-dusted tiles at the end.

d.text: Cold grey stone is the compound's body.

s.text: Lantern red, blue-grey night, and lamp gold.

a.text: Servant indigo and frost roof as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Warm period glow. The house is cold grey stone; the red lanterns are isolated marks of power, not ambient warmth.

volumes.title: Animation Worlds
volumes.eyebrow: Cinema · Vol VIII · Sourced from the Frame
volumes.h1: Four palettes from drawn and rendered worlds, built colour by colour.
preface: Animation chooses every colour deliberately — there is no 'available light' to fall back on. The palettes are designed top-down for mood, from Ghibli's gentle naturalism to the neon of cyberpunk anime and the comic-book chromatics of modern CG.
Each refuses the muddy realism of live action: animated colour is intentional, harmonised, and often joyful.

palettes.kicker: My Neighbour Totoro · 1988 · Studio Ghibli · the rural summer
palettes.title: A 1950s Japanese countryside in soft summer green, sky blue, and warm wood
palettes.source: Color from Ghibli's pastoral: lush summer green ricefields, soft sky blue, warm farmhouse wood, cream walls, and the grey-blue of Totoro.

swatches.name: Ricefield, lush summer green
swatches.note: The verdant countryside in full leaf.

d.text: Lush summer green is the countryside field.

s.text: Soft sky, warm wood, and cream walls.

a.text: Totoro grey-blue and signal red as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Garish cartoon brights. Ghibli's world is gentle, naturalistic, and harmonised — soft, not saturated kid-TV colour.

palettes.kicker: Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse · 2018 · the comic-book city
palettes.title: A halftone-textured New York exploding in magenta, cyan, and CMYK comic colour
palettes.source: Color from the comic-book grade: electric magenta, cyan glow, CMYK black, hot yellow, and a neon teal night.

swatches.name: Cyan glow, electric
swatches.note: The complementary blue half of the look.

d.text: Electric magenta leads the comic-book palette.

s.text: Cyan glow, indigo night, and hot yellow.

a.text: Comic-ink black and suit red as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Naturalistic CG. The film embraces print-process CMYK and chromatic aberration; realism erases its comic identity.

palettes.kicker: Akira · 1988 · dir. Otomo · Neo-Tokyo at night
palettes.title: A neon megacity of red bikes, amber light, and deep cyberpunk shadow
palettes.source: Color from Neo-Tokyo: deep night indigo, Kaneda's bike red, neon amber, electric teal, and a flash of psychic pink.

swatches.name: Kaneda's bike, signal red
swatches.note: The most famous red in anime.

swatches.name: Explosion, white-gold
swatches.note: The blast light over the city.

d.text: Deep indigo night is the city's field.

s.text: Bike red, neon amber, and electric teal.

a.text: Psychic pink and blast white as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Daylight flatness. Akira is a night film of glow-on-indigo; the red bike only sings against the dark city.

palettes.kicker: WALL·E · 2008 · Pixar · the abandoned Earth
palettes.title: A dust-orange ruined Earth of trash towers under a hazy sky, one green sprout
palettes.source: Color from the deserted planet: dust-orange haze, compacted-trash brown, hazy sky beige, WALL·E rust, and a single living-green sprout.

swatches.name: Dust haze, warm orange
swatches.note: The polluted atmosphere over the ruins.

swatches.name: Sprout, living green
swatches.note: The single plant that drives the plot.

d.text: Warm dust-orange haze covers the dead Earth.

s.text: Trash brown, beige sky, and robot rust.

a.text: Green sprout and EVE white as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Bright sci-fi optimism. The Earth half is a warm, dusty ruin; the one green sprout matters because nothing else is alive.

volumes.title: Dystopia & the Cold Future
volumes.eyebrow: Cinema · Vol IX · Sourced from the Frame
volumes.h1: Four palettes from broken futures, drained of warmth.
preface: Dystopian cinema desaturates: grey-green institutional light, concrete, and steel, with colour appearing only as warning or as a rare remembered warmth. The palette signals a world where something human has been removed.
Each refuses the vibrant blockbuster look: dystopia is muted on purpose, with one colour breaking through to mean hope or danger.

palettes.kicker: Children of Men · 2006 · dir. Cuarón · cin. Lubezki · the refugee road
palettes.title: A near-future Britain of grey rain, institutional green, and a single newborn's warmth
palettes.source: Color from the collapsing world: institutional grey-green, wet concrete, riot-fire orange, bus-window blue, and the warm skin of a baby.

swatches.name: Institutional grey-green
swatches.note: The drained colour of failing systems.

swatches.name: Newborn skin, warm
swatches.note: The baby — the one warmth left.

d.text: Institutional grey-green drains the whole film.

s.text: Wet concrete, dull blue, and drab brown.

a.text: Riot fire and newborn warmth as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Saturated action grade. Cuarón's future is grey and exhausted; warmth appears only as the newborn, deliberately.

palettes.kicker: The Matrix · 1999 · dir. Wachowskis · inside the simulation
palettes.title: A green-tinted simulated world against the cold blue of the real
palettes.source: Color from the two worlds: simulation code green, office grey-green, the cold blue of the real, black leather, and a phosphor-green glow.

swatches.name: Simulation, code green tint
swatches.note: The Matrix world's pervasive green cast.

d.text: Code-green tint marks the entire simulation.

s.text: Office green, cold-blue real, and black leather.

a.text: Phosphor code and red pill as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Neutral colour. The film codes its two worlds green and blue; removing the casts erases the simulation/real divide.

palettes.kicker: Mad Max: Fury Road · 2015 · dir. Miller · the desert chase
palettes.title: A post-apocalyptic chase in scorched orange day and supersaturated teal night
palettes.source: Color from the cranked grade: scorched orange desert, deep teal night, war-boy chrome white, blood red, and the green of the lost paradise.

swatches.name: Night, deep teal
swatches.note: The cranked blue of the desert dark.

d.text: Scorched orange dominates the daylight chase.

s.text: Teal night, tan dust, and chrome white.

a.text: Blood red and Green Place as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Naturalistic desert. Miller pushes orange and teal to the limit; a realistic grade flattens the kinetic spectacle.

palettes.kicker: Snowpiercer · 2013 · dir. Bong Joon-ho · the train cars
palettes.title: A train crossing a frozen world, each car a different colour from grim grey to lurid neon
palettes.source: Color from the carriage progression: tail-car grime grey, frozen-window blue-white, greenhouse green, sauna-car neon, and a protein-block brown.

swatches.name: Tail car, grime grey
swatches.note: The dark squalor of the poor cars.

d.text: Grime grey defines the oppressed tail cars.

s.text: Frozen blue-white, greenhouse, and warm yellow.

a.text: Sauna magenta and protein brown as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Uniform grade. Bong colours each car as a class; one consistent look erases the train's social gradient.

volumes.title: Pastel Romance & Whimsy
volumes.eyebrow: Cinema · Vol X · Sourced from the Frame
volumes.h1: Four palettes from the storybook films, soft and symmetrical.
preface: A strain of contemporary cinema embraces candy pastels, warm symmetry, and nostalgic colour as a deliberate aesthetic — romance and whimsy made of millennial pink, butter yellow, and mint. The palettes are gentle, harmonised, and consciously charming.
Each refuses the gritty realist grade: these films choose prettiness as meaning, not as decoration.

palettes.kicker: The Grand Budapest Hotel · 1932 era · dir. Wes Anderson · the lobby & funicular
palettes.title: A pink mountain hotel of pastel symmetry, plum uniforms, and confectionery detail
palettes.source: Color from Anderson's confection: hotel-facade pink, lobby plum-red, Mendl's box blue, butter gold, and a deep forest green.

d.text: Pastel pink is the hotel's dominant facade.

s.text: Plum livery, powder blue, and butter gold.

a.text: Forest green and pillbox purple as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Realist period grade. Anderson's world is a deliberate confection of symmetrical pastel; realism erases the doll's-house charm.

palettes.kicker: Amélie · 2001 · dir. Jeunet · Montmartre
palettes.title: A storybook Paris saturated in warm green and red, gold-lamp cosy
palettes.source: Color from the heightened Paris: pervasive green cast, warm red, gold lamplight, cream wall, and a saturated cobalt accent.

swatches.name: Cobalt accent, saturated
swatches.note: Doors and small props in blue.

d.text: A green cast colours the whole heightened Paris.

s.text: Café red, gold lamp, and cream walls.

a.text: Cobalt props and booth grey as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Neutral naturalism. Jeunet floods Paris with green-and-red warmth; a true grade removes the fairy-tale glow.

palettes.kicker: La La Land · 2016 · dir. Chazelle · the Griffith Park dusk
palettes.title: A musical Los Angeles of twilight magenta, primary-dress colour, and jazz-club blue
palettes.source: Color from the CinemaScope musical: twilight magenta sky, primary dress colours, night cobalt, warm-lamp gold, and a deep jazz red.

swatches.name: Night, deep cobalt
swatches.note: The blue of the evening exteriors.

d.text: Magenta-purple twilight leads the musical's palette.

s.text: Cobalt night, primary dresses, and lamp gold.

a.text: Emerald dress and jazz red as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Desaturated indie grade. Chazelle paints with saturated primaries and purple dusk; muting it kills the musical's joy.

palettes.kicker: Moonrise Kingdom · 1965 era · dir. Wes Anderson · the island summer
palettes.title: A 1960s New England island of khaki, butter yellow, and faded summer-camp colour
palettes.source: Color from the nostalgic summer: scout-khaki green, butter yellow, faded red, summer-sky blue, and a warm woodgrain.

swatches.name: Scout khaki, muted green
swatches.note: The Khaki Scouts' uniforms and tents.

d.text: Muted scout khaki is the summer's dominant tone.

s.text: Butter yellow, dusty red, and soft sky.

a.text: Warm woodgrain and pale sand as small reads.

palettes.refuses: High-saturation modern colour. Anderson's island is faded, warm, and nostalgic — a 1960s memory, not a bright present.

volumes.title: The Epic Landscape
volumes.eyebrow: Cinema · Vol XI · Sourced from the Frame
volumes.h1: Four palettes from the wide frame, where land is the lead.
preface: The landscape epic gives colour to scale: vast deserts, fields, and dunes shot in natural light where the land itself is the subject. The palettes are wide, warm, and elemental, with the human figure a small accent.
Each refuses the busy, cut-up modern look: these films breathe in single huge fields of earth and sky.

palettes.kicker: Lawrence of Arabia · 1962 · dir. David Lean · the Nefud desert
palettes.title: A mirage-shimmering desert crossing in pale gold sand under a white-hot sky
palettes.source: Color from the 70mm desert: pale sand gold, white-hot sky, robe cream, deep shadow brown, and a sliver of Arab-revolt red.

swatches.name: Desert sand, pale gold
swatches.note: The endless dunes in hard sun.

swatches.name: Shadow & rock, deep brown
swatches.note: The few dark forms in the heat.

swatches.name: Revolt flag, deep red
swatches.note: The Arab banner, a rare saturated note.

d.text: Pale sand gold is the overwhelming desert field.

s.text: White-hot sky, cream robe, and brown shadow.

a.text: Mirage silver and revolt red as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Saturated travel-poster desert. Lean's Nefud is bleached and pale, a furnace of gold and white, not rich orange.

palettes.kicker: Days of Heaven · 1978 · dir. Malick · cin. Almendros · the wheat at magic hour
palettes.title: A Texas-panhandle wheat farm shot almost entirely in golden magic-hour light
palettes.source: Color from the magic-hour harvest: ripe wheat gold, dusk-blue sky, lamp amber, deep field shadow, and a locust-fire orange.

swatches.name: Ripe wheat, golden
swatches.note: The endless harvest field at dusk.

swatches.name: Field shadow, umber
swatches.note: The long shadows across the wheat.

swatches.name: House silhouette, charcoal
swatches.note: The gothic house against the sky.

d.text: Golden wheat fills the magic-hour frame.

s.text: Dusk-blue sky, amber lamp, and umber shadow.

a.text: Locust fire and charcoal house as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Flat daylight. Almendros shot at magic hour for warmth and long shadow; midday light erases the film's gold.

palettes.kicker: The Tree of Life · 2011 · dir. Malick · the suburban memory
palettes.title: A 1950s Texas childhood in soft daylight, green lawns, and luminous curtains
palettes.source: Color from the memory grade: luminous lawn green, sun-curtain cream, sky blue, warm brick, and a backlit gold.

swatches.name: Lawn, luminous green
swatches.note: The childhood yard in soft sun.

swatches.name: Sky, soft blue
swatches.note: The wide Texas sky of memory.

d.text: Luminous lawn green leads the memory palette.

s.text: Sunlit cream, soft sky, and warm brick.

a.text: Golden backlight and shadow tree as small reads.

palettes.refuses: High-contrast drama. Malick's memory is soft, luminous, and backlit; hard contrast breaks the dreamlike recall.

palettes.kicker: Dune · 2021 · dir. Villeneuve · Arrakis at high sun
palettes.title: A monochrome desert planet of beige sand, brutalist shadow, and a flash of spice
palettes.source: Color from Arrakis: vast beige sand, brutalist concrete grey, deep shadow, suit-black, and the cinnamon-orange of spice.

swatches.name: Spice, cinnamon orange
swatches.note: The melange dust on the wind.

d.text: Pale beige sand is the planet's monochrome field.

s.text: Concrete grey, brown shadow, and suit black.

a.text: Spice orange and dusty sky as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Saturated space opera. Villeneuve's Dune is austere and beige; the only colour is spice, kept rare and earned.

volumes.title: Neon Neo-Noir
volumes.eyebrow: Cinema · Vol XII · Sourced from the Frame
volumes.h1: Four palettes from the neon night, where the city glows in the dark.
preface: Neo-noir trades the silver of classic noir for saturated neon: hot pink and electric cyan against wet black streets, the city lit by signage and screens. The structure is deep black violated by intense, artificial colour.
Each refuses the muted realist night: these films are luminous, electric, and synthetic — colour as atmosphere itself.

palettes.kicker: Blade Runner · 1982 · dir. Ridley Scott · the rainy LA street
palettes.title: A 2019 Los Angeles of acid rain, neon signage, and searchlight beams
palettes.source: Color from the original cyberpunk city: wet-black street, neon pink-magenta, searchlight cyan, smoky amber, and a cold blue interior.

d.text: Wet-black street is the field the neon glows on.

s.text: Magenta signage, cyan searchlight, and amber fire.

a.text: Cold interior blue and gold foil as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Naturalistic night. Scott's city only exists as neon-on-black; ambient light collapses its synthetic depth.

palettes.kicker: Drive · 2011 · dir. Refn · the LA night drive
palettes.title: A neon-noir Los Angeles of hot pink titles, cool blue night, and a white scorpion jacket
palettes.source: Color from Refn's synth-noir: cool blue night, hot-pink neon, the white satin jacket, amber streetlight, and a deep blood red.

swatches.name: Night, cool blue
swatches.note: The blue-black of the city drives.

d.text: Cool blue night is the film's dominant field.

s.text: Hot-pink neon, white jacket, and amber light.

a.text: Blood red and elevator gold as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Gritty desaturation. Refn's LA is a synth-pop dream of pink-on-blue; muting it removes the neon romance.

palettes.kicker: Enter the Void · 2009 · dir. Gaspar Noé · the Tokyo nightlife
palettes.title: A first-person drift through a Tokyo of saturated neon signage and strobing colour
palettes.source: Color from Noé's psychedelic Tokyo: black night, saturated neon pink, electric green, ultraviolet purple, and a club-strobe red.

swatches.name: Tokyo night, black
swatches.note: The dark the neon floats in.

d.text: Black night is the void the colour swims in.

s.text: Neon pink, electric green, and ultraviolet.

a.text: Strobe red and amber sign as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Realist nightlife. Noé's Tokyo is pure saturated glow on black; any naturalism erases the psychedelic drift.

palettes.kicker: John Wick · 2014 · dir. Stahelski · the Red Circle club
palettes.title: A neon-soaked nightclub gunfight in pools of blue, red, and ultraviolet
palettes.source: Color from the club shootout: deep blue wash, hot red, ultraviolet purple, black suit, and a teal pool-light.

swatches.name: Club wash, deep blue
swatches.note: The bath-house and club blue lighting.

d.text: Deep blue wash is the club's dominant light.

s.text: Hot neon red, ultraviolet, and black suit.

a.text: Teal pool-light and muzzle flash as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Flat action lighting. The film stages violence in saturated neon pools; even lighting removes the colour choreography.
